prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (Bannwarth et al., J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme at the GFN2-xTB level. The geometry was taken as-supplied from the provided .xyz file (no re-optimization). N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2).

Headline result: The iron center (Fe, atom 11, 1-based) is both the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191), with a dual descriptor of −0.030527354 (nucleophilic/HOMO-controlled character). All C and H atoms show much smaller Fukui indices (~0.012–0.063) and positive dual descriptors (~+0.0015), consistent with the symmetric D₅h/D₅d Cp-ring environment. Sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 (charge conservation checks passed). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. No warnings were reported by the engine.
